summary: Iteration 19 (Tier 4: see-other-campers + Raids) — 21/22 backend pytest pass in /app/backend/tests/test_iteration19.py. Frontend admin Raids tab + SpawnConfig 'show-group-positions' toggle + camper RaidScreen route all verified live on mobile viewport 414x896. CRITICAL backend bug found: POST /api/raids/{id}/throw returns 500 the moment a raid is defeated and NO participant gets the catch (UnboundLocalError: 'evolved_pl' + wrong collection lookup db.users instead of db.campers). All other raid mechanics (engage radius, group scoping, ball/damage table, participant set, max_hp seeding, /map/group-positions privacy + staleness + admin toggle) verified.

critical.issue: Defeat path 500s and never grants catches. server.py raid_throw() lines 3370–3406: loops over participants and calls `cu = await db.users.find_one({"id": cid})` — but campers are stored in db.campers, not db.users. Every iteration takes the `if not cu: continue` branch, so (a) NOT a single synthetic catch is inserted, (b) NOT a single +3 candy is awarded, and (c) `evolved_pl` is never assigned, causing `UnboundLocalError: cannot access local variable 'evolved_pl'` when the function tries to build RaidThrowResult(power_rolled=evolved_pl, ...). The throw that lands the killing blow returns HTTP 500 to the camper. Subsequent throws also fail with 400 'raid is defeated' but the raid is in a permanently broken state with NO participants having received the reward. Reproduce: seed any raid with max_hp=1 and one camper within 30m; one pokeball throw → endpoint returns 500 and db.catches has no row for that raid. Fix: change `db.users.find_one` → `db.campers.find_one` AND restructure so `evolved_pl` is computed once (or moved inside RaidThrowResult only when a participant exists). Also note: even after fix, `cu.get('username','Camper')` won't find anything in campers (campers have first_name/last_name/group_code, not username) — set caught_by from first_name+last_name or fallback to user passed into raid_throw.

minor.issue: duration_minutes=121 returns 400 ✓ but the bound is `> 120` strict. The error message 'duration_minutes must be 1..120' suggests an inclusive upper bound; either tighten the message ('1..120 inclusive') or accept 120. Passing 120 works; passing 121 fails — current behaviour matches docs.

Does not validate start_at < end_at semantics for raids with very long durations or past start_at. Past start_at is implicitly OK (and used by tests), but a start_at far in the future + duration of 1 will still be accepted with end_at also in the future — no validation that the window overlaps any meaningful play time.

Race condition between `find_one_and_update($inc damage_dealt)` and the subsequent status flip — two concurrent throws that both bring HP below 0 will each find the doc with status!=defeated and increment damage. The first one to reach the >=max_hp branch flips status, the second re-runs the synthetic-catch loop a second time (only mitigated by the BUG above masking it). Once the catch loop is fixed, add a guard: only run the reward branch when the previous status was not already 'defeated' AND use $set on status atomically inside the same find_one_and_update.

integration_issues.flow: Map peer overlay & raid marker on map
integration_issues.issue: Not directly verified on the Map page in this run (would require seeding a peer position + an active raid pin AND mocking google maps). The components GroupCampersOverlay and RaidsOverlay are mounted in MapPage.jsx (verified via grep, lines 505/507) and produce data-testid `peer-camper-<id>` and `raid-marker-<id>` respectively. RaidScreen route /raid/:raidId works end-to-end (raid-screen, raid-exit-btn, raid-hp-text, raid-throw-btn all rendered with HP 10/10 and disabled label 'Out of pokeballs' when wallet=0).

action_items: CRITICAL: Fix /api/raids/{id}/throw defeat path — change db.users.find_one → db.campers.find_one (~server.py line 3371), and ensure evolved_pl is initialised before the participant loop OR set RaidThrowResult.power_rolled to None when the loop iterates zero usable participants. Currently raid defeat 500s and grants nothing.

CRITICAL (paired): When constructing the synthetic catch_doc, derive caught_by from camper fields (first_name + last_name) since db.campers docs don't have a `username` key; using cu.get('username','Camper') always falls back to literal 'Camper' even after the collection-name fix.
MINOR: Add atomic defeat-state guard in find_one_and_update to prevent double-reward race when two throws land the killing blow within the same tick.

VERIFY (self-test): seed a raid with max_hp=1 + camper within 30m; throw one pokeball; (a) throw response should be 200 with defeated=true, (b) GET /api/catches should include a raid catch for the camper, (c) candies for that pokemon should have +3, (d) /api/raids/{id}/throw on the same raid should now return 400 'This raid is defeated'.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py raid_throw (~lines 3370-3408): WRONG COLLECTION — uses db.users instead of db.campers. Pure UnboundLocalError on every defeat. This is the single highest-impact bug in iter_19 because it silently breaks the headline feature; the 21 other passing tests give a misleadingly green picture. The fact that no test in the iteration's first wave caught this earlier suggests the codepath was never exercised before pytest hit it.

server.py raid_throw line 3406: power_rolled=evolved_pl referenced unconditionally. If participants list is non-empty but the inner loop continues for all (or is empty due to the addToSet race), evolved_pl is unbound. Either: declare `evolved_pl = None` before the loop, or only return power_rolled when at least one participant was credited.

server.py /map/group-positions (~line 3187): correctly filters by 10-min staleness using updated_at >= cutoff. Good. But the cutoff is computed once and passed as ISO; if camper_positions documents have their updated_at written with different ISO precision/timezone the comparison may behave inconsistently. Suggest storing as datetime objects or normalising to UTC Z.

server.py admin_create_raid (~line 3441): force-uppercases group_code via `(req.group_code or '').upper() or None`. Good — but does not validate that the group_code corresponds to an existing group (could create a raid for B99 with no campers and confuse admins). Add a quick existence check against db.campers.distinct('group_code').
frontend/src/pages/RaidScreen.jsx: throw-btn disabled-text logic correctly distinguishes 'Walk closer to the raid pin' vs 'Out of pokeballs'. Verified live: pokeballs=0 produced 'Out of pokeballs'.

frontend/src/components/GroupCampersOverlay.jsx polled every 10s (per file header comment) and RaidsOverlay every 8s — both reasonable, but note that two parallel polls plus the existing /spawn/current and /catches polls may push mobile devices over the desired 1 req/sec budget. Consider debouncing when the tab is hidden (visibilitychange listener).

success_rate.backend: 95.5% (21/22) — the 1 failure is the critical raid-defeat bug above
success_rate.frontend: Admin Raids tab + create form + status badge + SpawnConfig 'show-group-positions' toggle (toggle/save round-trip) + RaidScreen route + all camper raid testids verified ≈90% of frontend scope. Map overlays (peer-camper-*, raid-marker-*) not live-verified on the rendered map (would require Google Maps mocking) — components are correctly mounted and reachable per static analysis.

test_credentials: Admin: admin / Camp1993 via /api/admin/auth/login. Camper: B01 idx 5 + idx 6 (test_iteration19 backend), B01 idx 0 (Colton, Playwright). B02 idx 0 used for cross-group scoping.
seed_data_creation: Tests use motor to seed: (a) camper_positions (lat/lng/updated_at) for engage radius and stale filtering, (b) camper_wallets balances for ball-charge tests (correct collection: camper_wallets — NOT ball_wallets), (c) admin POST /admin/raids creates raids labeled TEST_*. Cleanup: every test deletes the raid it created via DELETE /admin/raids/{id}. Helper _force_raid_active() resets start_at/end_at via motor when a test needs a brand-new active raid without recreating.
context_for_next_testing_agent: iter_19 adds /api/map/group-positions, /api/raids/active, /api/raids/{id}, /api/raids/{id}/throw, /api/admin/raids[/end][/{id}] and SpawnConfig.show_group_positions. Engage radius is 30m around the raid pin (haversine via 111111m/deg). Damage table: pokeball=1, rayball=2, myrtleball=2, lunchball=3. Wallet collection is camper_wallets (NOT ball_wallets — common gotcha). Campers collection is db.campers (NOT db.users — see CRITICAL bug). To reach an active raid in tests, set start_at slightly in the past + status='scheduled' + ensure damage_dealt < max_hp. To force a fresh state on a single raid id, motor-update the raid doc (helper _force_raid_active in test_iteration19.py). Frontend admin tab id mapping: spawn config tab id is `spawns` (NOT `spawn-config`).
rca of the issue: The raid-defeat 500 has TWO compounding root causes: (1) `db.users.find_one({'id': cid})` always returns None for camper IDs because campers live in db.campers — every loop iteration short-circuits via `if not cu: continue`. (2) Because the loop body never executes successfully, `evolved_pl` (assigned at line ~3375 inside the loop) is never defined, so the very next line `power_rolled=evolved_pl` raises UnboundLocalError, which surfaces as HTTP 500. Net effect: the moment ANY camper deals the killing blow, the raid is marked defeated (line 3369 runs before the loop), the camper sees a 500, and zero catches/candies are issued. Fix is two-line: (a) change `db.users.find_one` → `db.campers.find_one` at the loop head AND (b) initialise `evolved_pl = None` before the loop OR move the RaidThrowResult construction inside the if-defeated branch only when participants exist. Also fix `caught_by` so it pulls from camper.first_name+camper.last_name, not the missing `username` field.
